title: Peppery Gooey Tunisian Romano Pepper Tagine
description: A flavorful and spicy Tunisian dish featuring slow-cooked Romano peppers, perfect for a brunch gathering. This FODMAP-friendly recipe combines the sweetness of peppers with the warmth of Tunisian spices.
instructions: Cut the tops off the Romano peppers and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic-infused oil, cumin, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the peppers to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Place the peppers in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot with a lid.
Pour in the chicken broth and cover the pot.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 2 hours, or until the peppers are tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the peppers hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, Tagine is cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid, but a Dutch oven works well too.
